Title: Botrytis cinerea infection accelerates ripening and cell wall disassembly to promote disease in tomato fruit

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall loosening/softening that facilitates ripening and pathogen colonization
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in ripening fruit; described as the main ripening-associated PG and highest expressed CWDE in red ripe fruit
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated by B. cinerea inoculation after 3 dpi in RT-qPCR; suppressed in AC-SlPG2A antisense fruit
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening stage; B. cinerea inoculation of mature green fruit
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Host SlPG2A contributes to pathogen establishment in unripe fruit; silencing reduces disease incidence and severity of most fungal strains on mature green fruit
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlPG2A is a major host ripening-associated polygalacturonase. 2. AC-SlPG2A fruit showed reduced disease incidence and severity upon mature green inoculation by wild-type and most mutant B. cinerea strains. 3. This supports a joint contribution of host and fungal pectin degradation to susceptibility.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Antisense suppression line analysis; disease incidence/severity assays in mature green fruit; expression analysis by RNA-seq/RT-qPCR context
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces pathogen susceptibility in mature green fruit; effect alone did not previously improve resistance in red ripe fruit
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to reduce postharvest susceptibility by moderating host pectin depolymerization, especially in unripe fruit contexts
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also written as SlPG2a/PG2A in the paper; antisense suppression line was in Ailsa Craig background

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes climacteric ripening and can enhance pathogen-associated susceptibility responses
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by B. cinerea inoculation in mature green fruit; lower in mock-inoculated fruit
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Prominent expression in inoculated mature green fruit through 3–6 dpi
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: B. cinerea infection of mature green fruit
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with premature ethylene biosynthesis during infection-driven ripening acceleration
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlACS8 was upregulated by B. cinerea inoculation, unlike normal ripening-associated reliance on other ACS genes. 2. RT-qPCR confirmed persistent strong expression in infected mature green fruit. 3. Its induction correlates with pathogen-accelerated ethylene production and ripening.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; RT-qPCR validation; ethylene phenotyping
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate host target for decoupling pathogen-triggered ethylene burst from normal ripening
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; GEO: GSE183836; prior dataset GSE148217; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as not normally central to ripening/System 2 yet induced by pathogen infection

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes climacteric ripening and susceptibility-associated ripening progression
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated by B. cinerea inoculation and during ripening
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Prominent expression in infected mature green fruit through later dpi by RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: B. cinerea infection of mature green fruit
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to premature ethylene biosynthesis and ripening acceleration during infection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlACO1, a System 2/ripening-associated ethylene biosynthesis gene, was upregulated by B. cinerea in mature green fruit. 2. RT-qPCR confirmed high expression in infected fruit relative to mock. 3. The data support premature activation of ripening ethylene biosynthesis by the pathogen.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; RT-qPCR validation; ethylene measurement
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to limit infection-triggered transition into ripening-associated System 2 ethylene production
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; GEO: GSE183836; prior dataset GSE148217; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as one of two System 2 genes induced by B. cinerea

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes infection-associated ethylene accumulation and ripening responses
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated by B. cinerea inoculation; not emphasized as normal ripening determinant
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong expression in infected mature green fruit from 3–6 dpi by RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: B. cinerea infection of mature green fruit
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with elevated ethylene production in infected unripe fruit
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlACO3 was prominently induced by B. cinerea inoculation. 2. RT-qPCR validated stronger expression in infected than mock-inoculated mature green fruit. 3. Its activation accompanies pathogen-induced ethylene burst and accelerated ripening.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; RT-qPCR validation; ethylene phenotyping
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate host susceptibility component in pathogen-induced ethylene biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; GEO: GSE183836; prior dataset GSE148217; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Listed among additional ACO paralogs induced by pathogen beyond canonical ripening genes

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit coloration and nutritional carotenoid accumulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High baseline expression in mature green fruit at 3 dpi and induced during ripening; later induction by B. cinerea detected by RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Greater expression in infected than mock fruit at later time points
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening and B. cinerea infection
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with accelerated color progression in infected mature green fruit
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlPSY1 is a ripening-associated carotenoid biosynthesis gene with high baseline expression in mature green fruit. 2. RNA-seq at 3 dpi did not show significant induction by B. cinerea, but RT-qPCR showed increased expression at later infection time points. 3. This supports contribution to accelerated ripening coloration during infection.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR validation; ripening color assessment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant if attempting to uncouple infection-triggered color development from normal carotenoid ripening programs
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; GEO: GSE183836; fruitENCODE comparison; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as possibly influenced by high baseline expression and/or indirect metabolic flux effects

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit coloration and nutritional carotenoid accumulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated during ripening; later induction by B. cinerea detected by RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in infected than mock fruit at later time points
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening and B. cinerea infection
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with accelerated carotenoid-related color progression in infected mature green fruit
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlZDS is a lycopene-pathway gene induced during ripening. 2. It was not significantly induced by B. cinerea at 3 dpi in RNA-seq but was induced at later time points by RT-qPCR. 3. Its expression pattern supports infection-associated acceleration of ripening coloration.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR validation; ripening color assessment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker/candidate for studying pathogen-triggered acceleration of carotenoid ripening
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; GEO: GSE183836; fruitENCODE comparison; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included among seven genes selected for targeted RT-qPCR confirmation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pectin backbone cleavage, fruit softening, and increased susceptibility to fungal colonization
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ripening-related CWDE; upregulated by B. cinerea after 3 dpi and at later time points
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in infected than mock fruit by RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to host pectin degradation linked to softening and susceptibility
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlPL is a ripening-related pectin-degrading enzyme. 2. It was induced by B. cinerea at later time points, despite weak/no significant induction at 3 dpi RNA-seq. 3. The paper cites prior evidence that silencing SlPL reduces susceptibility to B. cinerea in ripe fruit, supporting host CWDE hijacking.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR validation; literature-supported functional context
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing expression may alter normal softening/ripening texture traits
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising host target for reducing susceptibility by limiting pathogen-exploited pectin breakdown
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The direct functional reduction of susceptibility comes from prior work cited in discussion, not newly tested here

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream depolymerization step in pathogen-driven pectin breakdown
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes α-1,4-linked galacturonan in pectin backbone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to host cell wall disassembly and may trigger ripening-associated ethylene responses
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly degrades host pectin to promote susceptibility and generate signaling-active breakdown products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during infection of mature green tomato fruit; higher prominence in unripe fruit than ripe fruit or leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Infection of mature green versus red ripe tomato fruit; high humidity postharvest
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required together with Bcpg2 for virulence on unripe tomato fruit and for strong acceleration of ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bcpg1 showed high expression in infected mature green fruit. 2. Double knockout with Bcpg2 caused complete avirulence on mature green fruit but not on red ripe fruit. 3. Loss of Bcpg1 together with Bcpg2 greatly reduced pathogen-induced color change, ethylene production, and softening, showing failure to accelerate ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR of fungal gene expression; double-knockout mutant analysis; disease incidence/severity assays; ripening phenotyping; fungal biomass quantification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key virulence-associated pectin depolymerization step for infection of unripe fruit
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion strongly compromises growth, survival, and lesion formation on mature green fruit
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports targeting pathogen pectin depolymerases or reinforcing host pectin integrity to improve resistance in unripe/postharvest fruit
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (host/pathogen dual mapping); GEO: GSE183836; previous 1 dpi dataset GSE148217
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bcpg1 alone was previously known as a virulence factor, but this study shows complete loss of virulence on mature green fruit only when both Bcpg1 and Bcpg2 are absent

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream depolymerization step in pathogen-driven pectin breakdown
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes α-1,4-linked galacturonan in pectin backbone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to host cell wall disassembly and may participate in generating ripening-triggering pectin-derived oligosaccharides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly degrades host pectin to promote susceptibility and possibly produce ripening-triggering fragments
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during infection of mature green tomato fruit
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Infection of mature green versus red ripe tomato fruit; high humidity postharvest
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required together with Bcpg1 for fungal colonization of unripe fruit and efficient induction of ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bcpg2 was among four fungal pectin-degrading genes with high expression in mature green fruit. 2. Combined loss with Bcpg1 rendered B. cinerea completely avirulent on mature green fruit, while ripe-fruit infection remained possible. 3. Failure of the double mutant to accelerate ripening indicates Bcpg2 is necessary with Bcpg1 for this infection strategy.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR of fungal gene expression; targeted gene replacement; double-knockout mutant analysis; disease assays; ripening phenotyping; fungal biomass quantification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key virulence-associated pectin depolymerization step for infection of unripe fruit
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion together with Bcpg1 severely limits fungal biomass accumulation and long-term survival on mature green fruit
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Identifies pathogen PG pair as strategic targets for disease control and for breeding fruit with stronger pectin-based resistance
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (host/pathogen dual mapping); GEO: GSE183836; previous 1 dpi dataset GSE148217
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Compared with Bcpg1 and Bcpme1, Bcpg2 had been comparatively understudied prior to this work but emerges here as essential in combination with Bcpg1
